data.title: Polymarket to Temporarily Go Offline for V2 Upgrade
data.description: Polymarket is set to take its exchange offline for approximately one hour on April 22 to implement its V2 upgrade. According to NS3.AI, this update will replace the existing V1 trading contracts, order book, and collateral system. The new version will introduce pUSD as the platform's settlement asset on Polygon, replacing USDC.e. During the transition, all open limit orders will be canceled, but funds and existing positions will remain secure and unaffected.

data.title: Bank of Canada Governor: March CPI to Reveal Energy Price Impact
data.description: Bank of Canada Governor Tiff Macklem has indicated that the Consumer Price Index (CPI) for March will provide the first insights into the effects of energy prices on inflation. According to Jin10, Macklem emphasized the importance of understanding how fluctuations in energy costs are influencing overall price levels. This data will be crucial for policymakers as they assess the economic landscape and make decisions regarding monetary policy. The upcoming CPI release is expected to shed light on the extent to which energy prices are contributing to inflationary pressures in Canada.

data.title: Fed Governor Waller: March Overall PCE Inflation May Reach 3.5%
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller has indicated that the overall Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) inflation for March may reach 3.5%. According to Jin10, this projection reflects ongoing concerns about inflationary pressures in the U.S. economy. The PCE inflation measure is closely watched by the Federal Reserve as it provides insights into consumer spending and price changes. Waller's statement underscores the challenges faced by policymakers in balancing economic growth with inflation control. The Federal Reserve continues to monitor economic indicators to guide its monetary policy decisions.

data.title: Federal Reserve to Monitor Employment Data for Signs of Increased Pressure
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Waller has stated that the institution will closely observe employment data for any indications of heightened pressure. According to ChainCatcher, this announcement underscores the Fed's ongoing commitment to assessing economic conditions and adjusting monetary policy as necessary. The focus on employment data reflects concerns about potential shifts in the labor market that could impact broader economic stability.

data.title: Federal Reserve Official Comments on Employment Market Growth
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Waller has indicated that the current employment market's breakeven growth rate may be close to zero. According to ChainCatcher, this statement highlights concerns about the pace of job market expansion and its implications for economic stability.

data.title: Fed Governor Waller: Quick Resolution in Middle East Could Allow Rate Cuts Later This Year
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller has indicated that a swift resolution to the ongoing Middle East situation might pave the way for interest rate cuts later this year. According to Jin10, Waller emphasized the importance of geopolitical stability in influencing monetary policy decisions. He noted that while the current geopolitical tensions have created uncertainties, a rapid settlement could provide the necessary conditions for easing monetary policy. Waller's comments come amid heightened concerns over the impact of global events on the U.S. economy and the Federal Reserve's approach to interest rates. The Fed has been closely monitoring international developments as part of its broader strategy to maintain economic stability. Waller's remarks suggest that the Fed is prepared to adjust its policy stance if geopolitical risks diminish, potentially leading to rate cuts before the year's end.

data.title: AI TRENDS | GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.6 Achieve Anthropic-Style Bug-Finding Results
data.description: Vidoc Security has reported that AI models GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus 4.6 have successfully replicated Anthropic-style bug-finding results. According to NS3.AI, these models conducted scans that cost less than $30 per file, demonstrating that vulnerability discovery is becoming increasingly affordable and accessible. The study highlighted that both models consistently reproduced two bug cases across all three test runs, indicating the potential for widespread application in cybersecurity.

data.title: Bank of Canada Governor: High Energy Costs Impact Consumers and Business Investment
data.description: Bank of Canada Governor Tiff Macklem stated that rising energy costs are putting pressure on consumer spending and business investment. According to Jin10, Macklem emphasized that the central bank will not allow the increase in energy prices to translate into sustained inflation. The Bank of Canada remains vigilant in monitoring economic conditions to ensure that inflationary pressures do not become entrenched in the economy. Macklem's comments highlight the challenges faced by policymakers in balancing economic growth with inflation control amid fluctuating energy markets.

data.title: Bank of Canada Governor Acknowledges Risks of Premature Rate Hikes
data.description: Bank of Canada Governor Tiff Macklem has expressed concerns about the potential risks associated with raising interest rates too soon. According to Jin10, Macklem emphasized the importance of carefully assessing economic conditions before making any decisions on rate adjustments. He acknowledged that while there is a need to be cautious, the central bank is aware of the challenges that could arise from premature hikes. Macklem's comments come amid ongoing discussions about the timing and impact of monetary policy changes in Canada.

data.title: Swiss Central Bank Governor Reports Short-Term Inflation Rise
data.description: Swiss Central Bank Governor Schlegel has indicated a slight increase in inflation in the short term. According to Jin10, he noted that the medium-term inflation level remains largely unchanged.

data.title: Circle Founder Criticizes Traditional Proof-of-Work Mining
data.description: Circle founder Jeremy Allaire has expressed concerns about the energy consumption associated with traditional proof-of-work mining. According to NS3.AI, Allaire suggested that integrating AI inference with proof-of-work could lead to a more efficient consensus model. He also questioned the long-term relevance of Bitcoin, indicating uncertainty about its significance a decade from now.

data.title: Rumble Initiates Merger with Northern Data
data.description: Rumble has commenced its merger process with Northern Data, proposing an exchange of 2.0281 Rumble shares for each Northern Data share. According to NS3.AI, the merger, announced in November of last year, aims to provide Rumble with Northern Data's data center sites and thousands of GPU servers. Tether holds a majority stake in Northern Data and owns 30% of Rumble.

data.title: Digital Energy Council Joins Crypto Council for Innovation
data.description: The Crypto Council for Innovation has announced the inclusion of the Digital Energy Council as its first member dedicated to digital energy policy. According to NS3.AI, this new partnership aims to address policies related to energy development, grid resilience, U.S. competitiveness, and national security.

data.title: Iran Warns Against U.S. Naval Blockade
data.description: Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Baghaei stated on April 17 that a U.S. naval blockade against Iran would breach the ceasefire agreement. According to NS3.AI, Baghaei emphasized that Tehran is prepared to implement countermeasures if required.

data.title: Figure Counters Allegations of Blockchain Misrepresentation
data.description: Figure has responded to claims made by Morpheus Research, which suggested that Figure-related entities control over 65% of the HASH token on the Provenance Blockchain and that the company exaggerates its blockchain usage. According to NS3.AI, Figure clarified that funded loans, ownership transfers, and pledges are indeed recorded and executed on-chain. The company highlighted a 0.80% weighted-average delinquency rate across approximately $4.6 billion in securitized assets. Additionally, Figure reported executing over $1.15 billion in whole loan sales on its marketplace in March 2026.

data.title: World ID Protocol Receives Major Upgrade with Global Expansion
data.description:
World announced a significant upgrade to its World ID protocol on April 18, expanding its reach to approximately 18 million users verified by Orb across more than 160 countries. According to BlockBeats, this update is considered the most important iteration to date, introducing several practical applications. The upgrade integrates with Tinder to provide 'real person certification' for enhancing authenticity in dating, incorporates a deepfake verification feature with Zoom to confirm call participants as real humans, and launches the Concert Kit ticketing tool for identity verification and anti-scalping measures.

Additionally, World has released a standalone World ID app (beta version) as a cross-platform identity authentication tool and has open-sourced its SDK, allowing third-party applications to build their own authentication systems.

On the technical front, the upgrade introduces multi-key management, key rotation, account recovery, and session management mechanisms. It also proposes the concept of 'Human Continuity,' emphasizing continuous verification of the same real individual across multiple interactions, rather than just verifying devices or accounts.

In terms of ecosystem development, Reddit is exploring the use of World ID for identifying automated accounts, while Razer and Mythical Games have already adopted the standard in gaming scenarios.

Regarding the business model, World ID will charge fees to applications based on active users, while the user side remains free. Fees can be settled through on-chain wallets or third-party services, with support for automatic protocol-level distribution.

data.title: Alcoa in Talks to Sell New York Site to Bitcoin Mining Firm NYDIG
data.description: Alcoa is reportedly in advanced discussions to sell a former smelter site located in upper New York state to the bitcoin mining company NYDIG. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Alcoa's strategy to divest its dormant assets as part of its broader business plan. The potential sale underscores the growing interest in repurposing industrial sites for cryptocurrency mining operations. This move aligns with Alcoa's efforts to streamline its operations and focus on its core business areas. Details of the transaction, including the financial terms, have not been disclosed.

data.title: Trump said a deal between the US and Iran could be reached "within a day or two".
data.description: US President Trump stated that US-Iran talks are expected to take place this weekend, adding that the two sides "could reach an agreement within a day or two." In a phone interview, Trump expressed optimism about the progress of the negotiations, saying Iran "wants to meet and reach an agreement." He also emphasized that the naval blockade against Iran would not be lifted until a formal agreement is reached, but he hoped the Strait of Hormuz would be open to all countries. It is understood that although significant progress has been made in the negotiations, differences remain on key issues, including the suspension period for Iran's nuclear enrichment and the handling of its enriched uranium stockpile. The agreement also includes the possibility that the US might unfreeze approximately $20 billion in Iranian assets in exchange for Iran relinquishing its highly enriched uranium reserves. Furthermore, Trump stated that the agreement would "make Israel safer" and demanded that Israel cease its military strikes against Lebanon. Iran has not yet responded to these statements. (CCTV News)

data.title: Wall Street Firms Expand Digital Asset Access with New Bitcoin ETFs
data.description: Goldman Sachs has submitted an application for a Bitcoin income ETF, marking a significant move in the digital asset space. According to NS3.AI, this development comes as Morgan Stanley's recently launched Bitcoin ETF achieved a milestone, reaching $100 million in its first week. Additionally, Charles Schwab has announced that its clients can now trade Bitcoin and Ether within their accounts. These actions highlight the growing interest and involvement of major Wall Street firms in expanding access to digital assets.

data.title: HSBC Executive Departs Amidst Wave of Exits in Middle East Division
data.description: The head of HSBC's newly established banking division for the Middle East, North Africa, and Turkey has departed, according to sources familiar with the situation. Bloomberg posted on X that this departure is part of a series of recent exits at the bank. The reasons behind these departures have not been disclosed, and HSBC has yet to comment on the situation. The bank's Middle East division has been undergoing significant changes as it seeks to strengthen its presence in the region. The departure of key personnel could impact the bank's strategic plans and operations in these markets.
